# Transcrição: 5. 1 Month Contact – Mail or Personal Visit

- **Fonte:** 5. 1 Month Contact – Mail or Personal Visit
- **Duração:** 01:28
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:28]

30 days, one month, mail or personal visit. When you're going to finally confront that personal visit, somebody has to drive across town and go visit the customer. And when you do, the reason needs to be clear. By the way, you don't need an appointment. The way I would get an appointment is this. John, next Tuesday I'm going to be in your area. I tell you what, John, tomorrow, will you be at your office all day? Yeah, Grant, I'll be here all day. I'll call you tomorrow. I'll get with you tomorrow. I'll follow up with you tomorrow. Then you drive over here. See, the reason when you get there, you need to be clear. Just like on the phone. You walk in. Hey, the reason I'm here is I want to make sure we handled everything. That's why I came. See, once you tell people why you came, once you just bring it out overtly, like they're going to be like, oh, I can trust this guy. You don't need to come in there, him hauling, how's everything? Did you go on that fishing trip? You're making that stuff. You don't know what you're talking about. Just go in there and be crystal clear and honest. Be ethical. Hey, the reason I came by is to check on you. Number one, I came here to check on you. Good? We good? You like the purchase? You glad you did it? You getting any feedback? Okay, great. Now, second reason I came. Who do you know? Who do you know that would benefit from the same thing you did with me? Who do you know? I know you know people. You've been here. You're a State Farm agent. You've been here 30 years. You know everybody. Who do you know? Two reasons I went there. Personal visit, do it. Go see them. You'll never regret this personal visit and they'll remember you forever.
